signals.headline: IMF's January 2026 forecast projects U
signals.raw: IMF's January 2026 forecast projects U.S. GDP at 2.4% for 2026 (up from 2.0% in 2025), core PCE inflation at 2.6% by end-2026 (down from 2.9% in 2025), and minimal Fed rate cuts (fed funds from 3.6% to 3.4%), citing tariffs (7-8.5% effective rate) dragging growth and delaying 2% inflation target to 2027 amid energy risks from Iran conflict.[1]

signals.headline: Powell's December 10, 2025 statements called the economy "very unusual," attributing most inflation overshoot to Trump's
signals.raw: Powell's December 10, 2025 statements called the economy "very unusual," attributing most inflation overshoot to Trump's tariffs on goods (peaking Q1 2026), with Fed cutting rates by 25bps to 3.5-3.75% to support labor market amid overstated job data; services inflation cooling, non-tariff inflation near 2%.[2][3][4]
